paragraphs.body: 1. Main Point: What is the main point or topic of the article? Is the article's content relevant to the topic? Has the article used the correct Wikipedia style to add headings/subheadings, hyperlinks, and citations? The article’s main point is to give us an analysis of Spain’s national football team. This includes ways on how they could improve their abilities and academies to help keep their dominance on the world stage of football. The content’s article is relevant to the topic, and the article uses the correct Wikipedia style with the headings and subheadings. There are no hyperlinks and citations except for the end of the article which means that the article is still not ready for being published in Wikipedia.

2. Content: Is the article well-written-i.e. Is it concise, clear, and easy to read? How effective is the article’s use of transitional words? Is the article well-organized-i.e. broken down into paragraphs and sections that reflect the major points of the topic?

I would say that the article is well written and easy to read, and everything is divided into short sections that provide you with all the information needed. So, the article is well organized and broken down into paragraphs and sections that reflect the major points and topic. I see some use of transitional words like the word ‘while’, which is used multiple times in different paragraphs, which makes the paragraphs even easier to understand. Still, the author could have used different transitional words.

3. Tone: Has the article used formal academic language? Has the article used a third-person, neutral tone? Does the article have any grammatical or spelling errors? Does the article meet the length requirement (600 words)? Does the article include at least one heading or subheading? Are there hyperlinks to other Wikipedia articles?

The article uses formal academic language and uses a third-person tone to describe the Spanish national team and its players. I didn’t notice any grammatical or spelling errors so it seems like the article is well written. The article doesn’t meet the 600-word requirement (it has 534 words). There are no hyperlinks at all in the article.

4. Quoting and Paraphrasing: How effective has the author quoted and paraphrased information? Has the author used a signal phrase to introduce a quote/paraphrase and explain the quote/paraphrase in their own words?

The author’s quotations and citations seem effective in terms of relating to supporting the point he wants to make, and the citations are done correctly. The author also uses signal phrases like “the research done by Lopez and Martinez (2016)” to introduce a quote. Additionally, the author does explain the quotes he included in his own words.

5. Sources: Are all facts in the article backed up by a reliable secondary source of information? Has the author included at least 3 academic sources published within the past five years? Are the sources thorough - i.e. Do they reflect the available literature on the topic? Are the sources current? Are the sources written by a diverse spectrum of authors (such as historically marginalized individuals)?

All the sources seem to be reliable journals. Two of the sources were published within the five past years, but one was published in 2016, which is 9 years ago. The sources are thorough and reflect the topic effectively, and all but one of the sources are current. The sources are written by a spectrum of authors (each source has 2 different authors), but there are no marginalized individuals, as all the authors seem to be Spanish which is expected.

6. References: Are there better sources available, such as peer-reviewed articles in place of news coverage or random websites? (You may need to do some digging to answer this.) Check a few links in the list of references. Do they work? I didn’t seem to find sources that are relevant to the topic of Spanish national team analysis; all the topics I found where related to Spanish players' age and how it reflects on their skills. There doesn’t seem to be many sources on what the author wanted to write and he found the most effective ones even though one of the is a bit outdated.

7. Others: Do you have additional comments? For instance, has the author successfully integrated image and media files into the Wikipedia article? The author could have integrated some images of Spanish players or the Spanish team’s achievements to make the article seem more appealing and show the strength of the Spanish national team. Additionally, the article is still not in the style that Wikipedia accepts, so the author could include hyperlinks and in-text citations in the way that a Wikipedia article includes them. Other than these things the article seems to be well written and interesting in terms of the topic and information gathered.
